editorialSummary.text: Upscale seafood restaurant featuring wood fire cooking in a lodge-like setting with bay views.

text.text:
March, July 2021

Dining at Angler in March 2021 offered an exceptional case study in wood-fired culinary mechanics. Securing a seat with a direct view of the open-hearth fireplace is highly recommended, as it allows you to observe the kitchen’s precise temperature control firsthand. The service throughout the evening was excellent—polished, observant, and operating with a smooth, deliberate pace.

The menu landmarks demonstrate a flawless understanding of texture and salinity. The Parker House rolls arrive perfectly proofed, boasting a uniform golden glaze, paired with a remarkably deep seaweed butter. When dismantled and stuffed with their pastured chicken and pan jus, it yields an incredibly balanced, structurally rich bite. The wood-oven roasted chicken itself was spectacular—among the finest versions I have ever encountered, executed with pristine skin lacquering and served rustic style with the feet intact.

The accompanying elements maintain this high technical standard: the iconic whole radicchio salad balances bitter and sweet notes flawlessly, while the laminated potato showcases immaculate layer separation, anchored by a rich, sharp cheese sauce. Even the tabletop assets, like the heavy-duty custom knife, underscore the restaurant's sharp attention to detail.

Due to mid-pandemic protocols, the buffalo milk soft serve arrived with the warm caramel sauce on the side. Pouring the sauce independently added a compelling, interactive component to a structurally perfect dessert. Angler successfully strips away the rigid formality of fine dining without sacrificing a single drop of its technical execution. A compelling, highly recommended dining experience in San Francisco.

text.text: Loved the ambiance of this restaurant. Whoever is in charge of the music has great taste! The servers were extremely attentive but I expected more from the food. The presentation of each dish was great. The scallops & frog legs were delicious. We also ordered the ribeye steak medium well. When we received it, it still wasn’t cooked medium well so we sent it back. It came back just the same. At that point we just asked to have it boxed to go. This was disappointing considering the $154 steak price.

This was disappointing considering the $154 steak price.

text.text:
We went to Angler with my boyfriend and had a really great experience overall. The food was excellent. To start, we ordered the bluefin tuna and the bread—both were amazing. For our main course, we had the whole sea bream, which was absolutely delicious and a great size to share. We also shared the Angler potato and the greens salad, both very tasty and generous in portion.

Everything we tried was truly delicious. The only downside was the timing of the service. There was a 40-minute wait between our starter and the main course. We ended up asking our server what was going on since we had a reservation at a bar afterward and needed to leave. After the long wait, the fish finally arrived, but we had to eat it quickly so we wouldn’t be late, which was a bit of a shame.

That said, the overall experience was very positive. The food was outstanding, and the place has a really great vibe.

text.text:
The food here pretty amazing. Our only complaint is my wife found several of the dishes a little too salty, I thought it was great though.

We did the set menu and the total ended up being ~$475 for both of us with tip and tax.

I really loved the bluefin tuna with the rice cracker. I can't believe how much flavor they packed into that dish. This was both our favorite dish. For me it was very close with the uni toast, I honestly don't know how to describe it other than it being a very savory and rich flavor. I do agree that one was a tad bit too salty, otherwise it would have been the best dish for me by a large margin.

If you have the chance to come here for a special occasion to try it yourself I really recommend it. We'll definitely be coming back to try it again in the future.

text.text: We had an incredible dining experience. Everyone was so warm and inviting, and several staff came up to us to wish us happy anniversary. The food was delicious and presenting beautifully. The wait staff helped guide us through the menu and helped me figure out an excellent cocktail because I was so sick of wine after a Napa trip. Thank you!!

Thank you!!
